args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(arrivals): add long-press tooltip to LineBullet showing line info

300ms hold on any line bullet shows a tooltip with the full line name,
division (A/B), and express/local classification. Uses pointer events
on a wrapper span so the tooltip works regardless of onClick state.
Suppresses click after long-press to avoid both handlers firing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content: Pushed successfully. Now close the bead:

args.command:
br close bf-445l --body "Added long-press (300ms) gesture to LineBullet component that shows a tooltip with full line name, division (A/B), and express/local classification.

## Retrospective
- **What worked:** Wrapping the button in a \`<span>\` and attaching pointer events to the wrapper cleanly solved the disabled-button problem — pointer events fire on the wrapper regardless of the button's disabled state, so long-press always works even when there's no onClick.
- **What didn't:** Nothing blocked; straightforward implementation.
- **Surprise:** The existing \`getLineMetadata\` function was already exported from \`@mta-my-way/shared\` with all the needed fields (longName, division, isExpress), so no shared-package changes were required.
- **Reusable pattern:** For touch gestures on potentially-disabled buttons: put the gesture handlers on a \`position: relative inline-flex\` wrapper span, render the tooltip as an absolutely-positioned \`pointer-events-none\` child, and use \`longPressFired.current\` to suppress the click event that fires after a long-press release."
